meta.component: Phase 0.4 — numeric weights + threat-weight formula (D-002, D-009).
meta.purpose: Single home for every constant the engine uses. 'No magic numbers in engine code' (PLAN Shared Contracts: Threat weight). The engine imports these; it never inlines a number.
meta.principle: Mechanics live in the KBs (durable). STRENGTH lives in the patch overlay (volatile). These weights are the durable MAPPING from KB signals -> a score; per-patch strength enters only via patch_trend_multiplier, which defaults to neutral (1.0) until the patch overlay is synced (Q-002).
meta.formula_ref: See threat_weight_formula and objective_formula below — both written out, per the 0.4 acceptance criterion.
meta.compliance: These weights drive PICK/SWAP scoring only. The counterability->weight map is a THREAT-priority signal, NOT a ban score (the ban machinery it superseded is quarantined in counter_kb _archival_do_not_use, D-004). The inverse of this ordering is never surfaced (D-006).

counterability_weight._doc: How hard an enemy hero is to answer via a pick = how much THREAT priority it carries. LOW counterability => hard to answer => HIGH threat weight. This is the inverse of the old ban_value, repurposed as pick-priority (D-004).

conditional_resolution._doc: counterability 'conditional' heroes (Moon Knight, Spider-Man, Squirrel Girl, Wolverine, Jeff) only count as covered if OUR pool can deliver a required mechanism (Phase 1.3). If the pool delivers it, treat as covered at full weight; if not, escalate toward the 'low' (hard) weight because it is effectively unanswered.

patch_trend_multiplier._doc: trend -> multiplier on a hero's threat weight. Comes from the patch overlay (Q-002). Until the overlay is synced, every hero is neutral (1.0). The engine multiplies counterability_weight by this. Range clamps prevent a single volatile patch signal from dominating durable mechanics.

playstyle_delta._doc: raise/lower from comp_gap_model.playstyle_modifiers -> additive deltas on a comp-function's gap priority once an archetype is detected. Applied AFTER the base ideal-shortfall weight.

function_gap_weight._doc: Base weight of an UNFILLED comp function = ideal_providers shortfall x per_missing_provider, with a critical bonus when a function with ideal>=1 has ZERO providers (comp_gap_model.gap_rules.critical_gap). A candidate earns this gap weight for a function it DECLARES (hero_functions), and a discounted fraction for one reached only via the mechanism->function crosswalk (a weaker 'kit touches this role' signal). A single candidate's total function-fill credit is capped (per_candidate_fill_cap) so one pick can't claim credit for many unrelated gaps at once (D-009 double-counting guard).

objective_penalties._doc: The negative terms of the D-009 constrained objective. Subtracted from a candidate pick/swap's score.

threat_weight_formula.conditional_note: If counterability == 'conditional', substitute conditional_resolution.covered_weight or .uncovered_weight for the base, decided by Phase 1.3 pool-mechanism intersection, THEN multiply by trend.
threat_weight_formula.worked_example: Gambit: counterability=low -> 1.0; trend defaults neutral -> x1.0 => threat_weight 1.0 (highest tier). Black Widow: counterability=high -> 0.35; neutral => 0.35 (low priority; just counter-pick her).

objective_formula._doc: D-009: single constrained objective, exhaustive over the comfort pool. NOT greedy set-cover. Maximize this score over every legal candidate (in pool, legal role slot, <=1 change); pick the max; emit Hold if no candidate clears the min_score gate.
objective_formula.expression: score(candidate) = SUM[covered enemy threat_weight] + SUM[filled comp function_gap_weight (incl. crosswalk credit)] - introduced_vulnerability*(candidate's own countered_by present on enemy roster) - clash_penalty*(archetype clash) - redundancy_penalty*(over-provided function)
hard_constraints: candidate fills a legal role slot
<=1 change vs current comp in live mode
objective_formula.hold_gate: If max score < min_confident_score, emit Hold (D-006) rather than force a marginal pick.
